finding: Sessions past 23:00 occurred on 5 of 12 active days in the window (Apr 4, 7, 10, 11, 14). On Apr 7, four sessions ran between 23:10-00:01 across intender and claude-config. On Apr 11, six sessions ran 23:27-23:37. On Apr 14, work continued until 00:29 across whisper-writer, marketplace, and assistant projects. A late-night-warn hook exists (triggers after 20:00 with 5h+ active time) but it only fires once per hour and does not prevent new sessions from starting. The recurrence across days suggests the hook's nudge is insufficient to change the pattern.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook at ~/.claude/late-night-warn.py — fires after 20:00 with 5h+ active time, with 60-min cooldown. Appears insufficient given recurrence.

finding: Two days in the window showed manic-mode signatures: Apr 11 had 36 sessions spanning 15.3 hours (08:18-23:37) across jhana, weekend/intender, and claude-config simultaneously, with parallel sessions running continuously from afternoon through late evening. Apr 14 had 30 sessions spanning 15.8 hours (08:40-00:29) across 8 different projects (nixos, marketplace, dotfiles, autodoro, whisper-writer, assistant, claude-config). Both days show scope escalation (starting new projects in the evening) and sustained parallel execution — classic manic-mode indicators per the wellbeing category definition.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook exists but only addresses the late-night component, not the marathon duration or scope escalation pattern.

finding: From Apr 7 to Apr 14, 7 out of 8 days had active Claude sessions (only Apr 8 was off). Within this streak, four days exceeded 12-hour work spans (Apr 7: 13.9h, Apr 9: 13.2h, Apr 11: 15.3h, Apr 14: 15.8h). The user's stated wellbeing goals include 'mindful forest walks, breaks from screen' and 'mix productive output with being very human: present, mindful, high wellbeing.' The sustained intensity with minimal recovery days sits in tension with these goals. The single rest day (Apr 8) was followed immediately by a 13.2-hour day.
existing_mitigation: None found beyond the late-night hook. No mechanism addresses day-over-day intensity accumulation.
